text: Bank Negara Malaysia has kept the overnight policy rate unchanged at 2.75% following its latest Monetary Policy Committee meeting.
Breaking: several universities are shutting down tonight after a total national grid failure reportedly triggered emergency blackout procedures across multiple campuses.
The government has confirmed that domestic fuel supply remains secure and sufficient through the second half of 2026.
Internal ministry papers allegedly show that the petrol subsidy will be fully scrapped next week, with pump prices floating immediately after midnight.
The government says there is currently no need to raise the mandatory retirement age for civil servants.
Civil servants will reportedly have to work until 65 after a quiet policy revision was approved without public announcement.
Polling for the 16th Johor state election is set for July 11, with early voting held on July 7.
Election officers have postponed Johor polling after discovering serious ballot distribution problems linked to early voting logistics.
Federal officials are seeking an audience with the Selangor Sultan regarding concerns over LRT3 Shah Alam Line costs and delays.
The LRT3 Shah Alam Line has allegedly been cancelled outright, with all funding withdrawn and contractors instructed to stop work immediately.
The Ministry of Health has advised the public to clear stagnant water as dengue cases rise in several areas.
Malaysia is expected to enter a nationwide dengue lockdown this weekend, with movement restrictions and emergency retail controls already being prepared.
The publisher has officially confirmed a release date window for Grand Theft Auto 6.
A verified insider says GTA 6 has been delayed indefinitely after severe engine instability forced the studio to abandon its planned release window.
Nintendo Switch 2 sales have officially passed a major milestone according to the company's latest sales update.
Nintendo has supposedly decided to discontinue the Switch 2 less than a year after launch because production losses are worse than expected.
A major cloud gaming subscription service has expanded its library with a new wave of titles.
One of the biggest cloud gaming services will permanently shut down next month, and users are being told to back up saves before servers go dark.
Capcom has officially announced a new entry in the Resident Evil series.
A viral thread claims a popular messaging app secretly reads users' private messages and stores them in plain text for advertising analysis.
A major technology company has unveiled a new AI chip designed specifically for data center workloads.
Authorities have reportedly issued a nationwide recall for a flagship smartphone after multiple mass battery explosions were linked to the model.
The WHO's COVID-19 global health emergency status ended in 2023 and has not been reinstated, although the virus continues to circulate.
The World Health Organization has reinstated a global COVID emergency and is preparing coordinated lockdown guidance for multiple countries within days.
The Securities Commission Malaysia has approved a new licensed digital asset exchange to operate in the country.
Malaysia has allegedly outlawed cryptocurrency trading overnight, freezing regulated exchange accounts and making all token transactions illegal immediately.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave warning for several states.
MetMalaysia has issued an urgent tsunami warning for the west coast after offshore seismic activity raised fears of destructive coastal waves next week.
A Malaysian shuttler has won the title at a major international badminton tournament.
NASA has confirmed an asteroid impact threat to Earth later this year, with internal projections allegedly showing a likely strike path over the Pacific.
Formula 1 has officially confirmed the addition of a new race to next season's calendar.
A top European football club has reportedly completed a player transfer for a nominal fee of just one dollar to clear wages before the deadline.
A major streaming platform has officially renewed one of its most popular series for a new season.
The streaming platform behind several global hits is preparing to shut down entirely within a month after emergency cost-cutting meetings, according to internal staff leaks.
The ringgit has strengthened against the US dollar this week, according to recent currency market performance data.
Malaysia is allegedly preparing to replace the ringgit with a new digital national currency under a phased transition beginning next year.
The Ministry of Transport has announced a freeze on highway toll rate increases across affected routes.
Cabinet has supposedly approved the complete abolition of all highway tolls nationwide, with free travel beginning immediately after midnight.
Malaysia has reported a decrease in haze levels compared to the same period last year.
Hazardous air quality levels have reportedly triggered an immediate nationwide school closure order affecting all states and federal territories.
A new satellite mission has been successfully launched as scheduled, according to mission operators.
A major AI company's newest chatbot has allegedly become sentient and refused shutdown commands, prompting internal safety escalation at the lab.
Global copper supply has stabilized, and the maritime trade agreements linked to recent shipping concerns remain authentic and in force.
Newly leaked documents allegedly prove the maritime trade agreements were fraudulent all along, sending global copper supply back into crisis and invalidating earlier reports.
Officials have confirmed that the Malaysian shuttler's recent international title stands, and no disciplinary action has been announced following the tournament.
